label: Mikaela Moskowitz - Business Process Analyst Global Services & Support
profileText:
current_status: Early stage professional (< 5 years)
university: Northeastern University

target_roles: Business Operations Analyst; Process Transformation / Process Improvement Analyst; Strategy Analyst (Internal / Corporate Strategy); Post-Merger Integration (PMI) Analyst / Associate; Internal Consulting / Transformation Analyst; Operations & Strategy Analyst
adjacent_roles: Business Analyst; Internal Consultant or Transformation Enablement Analyst; Change Management or Organizational Development Analyst; Implementation / Solutions Analyst (non-sales); International or Cross-Border Operations Analyst
target_industries: Asset Management; Consulting (Management/Strategy); Financial Services (General); Private Equity; Venture Capital; Wealth Management; Professional Services (General); FinTech; eCommerce; Commercial Real Estate; Real Estate Development; Real Estate Private Equity; Healthcare Services; Hospitality; Travel and Tourism; Manufacturing; Aerospace and Defense; Supply Chain and Logistics; Startups (Growth Stage); Small/Founder-Led Companies
specific_companies: Accenture; Deloitte; SAP; ServiceNow; Airbnb
do_not_want: Sales or commission-based roles; highly technical finance roles; data or analytics roles requiring programming; roles with primary focus on coding software engineering or technical development; aggressive high-pressure finance environments with limited collaboration or mentorship; administrative roles without strategic cross-functional work
openness_to_non_obvious_entry_points: Somewhat open
location_preferences: Boston; Miami; Orlando; Charlotte; Chicago; East Coast
timeline_for_starting_work: Fall 2026
strongest_skills: systems thinking; communication; cross-functional coordination; process improvement; explaining complex concepts clearly; working in ambiguity; bridging finance and departments
job_search_concerns: interview performance
feedback_style: Balanced (honest but encouraging)
resume_paste: Mikaela Moskowitz 617.306.5288 | mikaelamoskowitz55@gmail.com | linkedin.com/in/mikaela-moskowitz SUMMARY Process transformation professional focused on modernizing finance accounting and operational systems in complex regulated environments. Proven record leading end-to-end process redesign close optimization and cross-functional change initiatives across global organizations. U.S. citizen actively relocating to Orlando, FL. CORE COMPETENCIES Finance & Accounting Process Optimization | End-to-End Process Redesign & Standardization | Cross-Functional Transformation & Stakeholder Alignment | KPI Definition Reporting & Performance Tracking | Process Mapping Documentation & RACI Design | Change Management | ERP & Systems Enablement | Operational Controls Governance & Audit Readiness | Post-Merger Integration & Scalable Operating Models RELEVANT EXPERIENCE TTIS Germany GmbH Berlin Germany Post-Merger Integration Specialist Dec 2024 ? Present Lead cross-functional post-merger integration of two acquired entities, standardizing finance HR purchasing and administrative processes to improve scalability and control. Redesigned and operationalized the month-end close, establishing clear RACI ownership and reducing close cycle time by ~67%. Re-engineered end-to-end project lifecycle from bid through billing, eliminating fragmented workflows and control gaps. Built standardized business case and pricing framework, replacing ad hoc estimates with finance-supported auditable models. Implemented first budgeting and approval framework for acquired entities, creating a proactive expense management process. Standardized cross-entity reporting, eliminating duplication and reducing errors by ~50% while strengthening data governance. Redesigned forecasting and revenue-tracking processes to improve margin pipeline and cash-flow visibility. Partner with IT to translate current- and future-state processes into technical specifications, enabling ERP automation across pricing forecasting and billing. Serve as primary liaison between operations and leadership, driving change management and cross-functional alignment. Developing a scalable post-merger integration playbook to support future acquisitions and system rollouts. Ernst & Young Munich Germany US Real Estate Tax Compliance Analyst Jan 2024 ? Aug 2024 Supported U.S.-German tax compliance for corporate and partnership clients with > ?200M in assets, ensuring accuracy across cross-border reporting requirements. Redesigned onboarding materials and peer training workflows, accelerating new-hire ramp-up and improving team productivity. Coordinated across international teams to resolve complex cross-border compliance tax issues, improving turnaround time by 30%. Tip-Top Industrial Solutions Berlin Germany Merger Organization & Process Specialist May 2016 ? Aug 2023 Supported post-merger organizational and process integration for LAWA/WKH, aligning budgeting and reporting structures with parent-company standards. Documented and standardized ERP workflows to support system transition and staff onboarding. Performed budget variance analysis on 24M+ RUB infrastructure projects, partnering with senior leadership in decision making. Spearheaded ERP (1C) implementation, helping automate workflows, develop training materials and organize system documentation. EDUCATION Northeastern University D?Amore-McKim School of Business Boston MA Dec 2024 Bachelor of Science in International Business: Finance | Minor in History cum laude SKILLS & INTERESTS Tools: Excel (X/V Lookup, Index Match, Pivot Tables & Charts, Sum/Count/Average Ifs, Power Query, Data Validation, Scenario Tables & Forecast Models). Financial & Process Systems (ERP (1C), GoSystems, CSV/API/ASCII Integrations) Languages: English (Native), Russian (Fluent), German (Intermediate), Spanish (Beginner)
cover_letter: 
extra_context: Looking to move into a more clearly defined specialized role where she can build depth. Learns best in environments with strong peer groups mentorship collaboration and clear progression.
jobText: The Business Process Analyst for Global Services & Support is responsible for analyzing designing and optimizing end-to-end service and support processes to translate service strategy into coordinated cross-functional execution. The role acts as a bridge between Global Services & Support Finance Manufacturing and IT, ensuring operational needs are converted into scalable repeatable and well-governed systems. Responsibilities include owning end-to-end process design documentation and governance; identifying inefficiencies and friction points across workflows; facilitating alignment when tradeoffs arise; ensuring processes support internal controls audit readiness and operational rigor; partnering with stakeholders; serving as translator between business users and IT; owning intake and prioritization for system enhancements; translating business problems into functional requirements; collaborating with BizOps Insights on metrics; overseeing UAT; driving structured change management; establishing process governance standards; and ensuring documentation controls and ownership are defined. Qualifications include bachelor?s degree in business operations or related field, experience owning or materially contributing to enterprise-scale process design and/or system implementations, experience in scaled audit-aware or regulated environments, strong working knowledge of CRMs ERPs MRPs and related systems, ability to work through ambiguity and drive alignment, and exceptional communication organization and attention to detail.

adjacentRoles: Internal Consultant or Transformation Enablement Analyst
Change Management or Organizational Development Analyst
